data.epi_content:
The Crown Prince handed me a container of powder that supposedly ignites well and a potion that would prevent me from feeling pain, then sent me back to Evan.

Looking up at the sky, the moon is truly beautiful.

Its round shape, half-hidden by clouds yet still shining, looked so brilliant.

How envious I am.

"So, what did His Highness want to talk to you about?"

"Well, my family's completely collapsed.

So now who's going to take it all?"

"Ah."

"That's all it was."

I spoke as if telling them not to worry about it.

They both nodded, seemingly satisfied with the answer and showing no signs of discomfort, as if already accustomed to such things.

While I was talking with the Crown Prince, Evan must have bought drinks because he handed me a coffee filled with ice.

I accepted it with a broad smile and began drinking.

As soon as Vivian and Evan became distracted with their conversation, I dropped the smile I had been forcing.

The three of us sat on the bench where I had just been sitting with the Crown Prince.

I asked what I'd been wondering.

"Hey, how long are you two going to keep dragging me around like this?"

At my words, Evan seemed taken aback by my sudden rebellion after so long, and spoke in a placating tone.

"Erica, you've been doing so well lately, why suddenly..."

"I'm not your pet.

Nobles might see commoners that way, but I'm still a person.

How long do I have to keep pretending to be happy, smiling, and fawning over you both?"

No answer came.

The atmosphere, which had felt somewhat warm until just now, began to sink into the water along with the moonlight.

It's a bit sad that the only listener taking this conversation seriously is the carp.

"Please just let me go now.

It hurts. Every time you two are happy in front of me.

You say we're dating, but nothing's changed from when we were just friends.

Did you just say that to torment me? Hey, Evan. Answer me."

"Erica, remember last time when you cursed at Vivian here, and at me too, begging us to let you go?

If I hadn't used healing magic then, you would have died from your skull caving in and blood pooling in your brain.

When you act like that in public too, how could I possibly leave you alone?"

That's a shame.

I could have died.

You saved me because it would be sad if your pet died.

"For the past month, I've been quite obedient, haven't I?

Give me back my father's belongings. Let me go. I just hate being with you two.

Vivian, who's dating Evan despite knowing I like him, and you, Evan—you're both just terrible.

I don't understand how this girl still likes you even if she died several times."

As I continued speaking, Evan began to slowly move his fingers.

I watched his right hand carefully while pulling out the half-burned cigar the Crown Prince had given me and placing it between my lips.

I lit it and took several long drags, inhaling and exhaling the smoke repeatedly.

"What, hearing things you don't like makes you want to put me to sleep again?"

"That's not it."

"Vivian, will you please let me go now?

I'm not your attachment doll.

As I said before, I can't be a replacement for your dead father either."

Death is something only an individual can possess and keep, yet how cruel are those who take even that away!

If God had taken death from me, leaving me unable to die even when I should, I would wail at the absurdity—but to prevent me from even reaching the process of death is simply evil.
data.epi_content2:
"Isn't a month long enough?"

No one answers my question.

They must have felt it too.

That I'm cumbersome, unhelpful, and useless.

But the fact that I was Evan's childhood friend, someone he might have married, and Vivian's first female friend kept them from letting me go.

I'm not saying they're evil.

They're just too immature to know how to let go.

It's always hard to break up with your first love.

Unless one party develops a major flaw.

And it's always a bit wistful afterward.

But not many people think of imprisoning and keeping their ex-lover afterward.

Some might, but most just leave it as a painful memory and move on.

I could say my situation is somewhat different, but it's similar, isn't it?

Just keep me and what does it matter if I live or die?

"It's time to end this abnormal situation.

Or just be honest. Say you need a pet.

Say you need an obedient little dog."

I said to Evan.

Then I looked at Vivian and continued.

"Say you need an attachment doll that's obedient, clean, and pretty.

Nothing makes you happier than hugging it to sleep every night, all wrapped up under the name of friendship."

"That's not... what I..."

"I know that's not what you mean.

But you've already crossed the line, way too far.

You two are killing me every day.

I'm just withering away day by day.

I'm not even bothered by Lydia's clumsy attempts to hurt me because she's afraid of you two.

Every time you drag me around, laugh together, show me how happy you are, and try to be considerate, my heart aches and it's too painful.

And every time I see you, Vivian, sleeping with that pleased look on your face, I feel like I'm going insane."

So, the conversation ends here.

If you don't like it, I'll just burn to death as the Crown Prince suggested, on a day of my choosing.

It's too painful when you give me this excessive attention when you normally don't give me any.

No, not excessive—it's the halfhearted way you give it that makes me feel like dying.

"Or, Vivian, should we pretend nothing happened like you suggested?

I've already said everything, but should we act like nothing happened?"

I approached Vivian and gently caressed her cheek as I spoke.

She trembled slightly, giving me the fresh feeling of having caught a fleeing rabbit.

"Let's go back.

Put on my leash, feed me, and praise me occasionally when I behave well.

I'm your pet, after all."

Evan and Vivian had no intention of letting me go.

They didn't even try to compromise with me.

Just because I'm abnormal.

Because they never know when I might suddenly die.

"I've lived and died many times, but this place is quite new. Painful, but new."

With that, I took Vivian's hand, leaving Evan behind as we headed toward the dormitory.

I looked back once.

Evan was holding his forehead, frowning deeply in thought.

Of course, it must be difficult for him.

If he lets me go, I might just die suddenly.

But keeping me means watching me wither away slowly.
data.epi_content3:
But why should I care about that?

You just need to keep living happily with Vivian like you are now.

When I say things like this, this girl retreats inside herself.

To a place where no sound can be heard, no emotion can be felt, completely submerged.

What can I expect from this foolish girl who couldn't even drink poison properly and died slowly of illness?

I just hope she gets better, little by little.

...

Another night passes, and Vivian inevitably starts taking me to class again.

"Hey, Vivian. Are you really going to keep dragging me around like this forever?"

Vivian doesn't answer.

Perhaps she cried a little in bed last night.

Her eyes are slightly red.

"When you have children with Evan later, when you have another family, will you still keep me around?

It's not realistic. This problem would be solved if you just let me go."

"How... how could I do that..."

"Why not? You're the protagonist of this world.

Everyone loves you. Your abilities are incredibly outstanding.

So what am I beside you but a blemish?

You detestable little girl."

Saying that, I pushed Vivian aside and ran off somewhere.

Vivian fell back weakly and followed after me.

Her heart must be tired, because although she followed me physically, she didn't call for me to stop.

Sure, living together like this was painful for me, but Vivian must have been suffering too, dealing with my hysteria.

Exhausted in body and mind, to the point where she couldn't bear to let me go.

Yes, like those lunatics in nursing homes who act like 2-year-olds in 20-year-old bodies.

They were actually easier to handle.

When something made a loud popping sound, they would soil themselves, tremble, crawl under the bed, cover their ears, and lie face down.

Looking at their ridiculous figures and at Vivian following me, I burst into laughter.

It was an incredible feeling of liberation.

Yes, it's always been like this.

I sprinkled the pungent-smelling powder the Crown Prince had given me all over myself.

Vivian must have broken the jewel Evan had given her, because in the distance, Evan was running quickly toward us.

Whew, running after so long makes me out of breath.

Tired, I lay spread-eagle in the middle of what resembled a plaza in the Academy.

People's gazes fixed on me.

Eyes wondering if I'd finally gone mad, or why I was making such a spectacle now.

So I got up.

"Evan, looks like you ran hard to get here."

"Erica, let's just talk about this.

I can't give you everything you want, but we can work something out."

"When I tried to talk like this before, you two—Evan and Vivian—just dismissed it as the ravings of a lunatic.

It's okay. Even though we're in this terrible relationship now, things will go back to how they were.

I don't want to meet again, but let's meet again."

I'll go back in time once more.

I don't like pain. Will this potion really prevent me from feeling pain?

When the end of my arm burned before, I just wanted to die.

The taste is sweeter than I expected.

What should I do next time?

I've already tried being a pet, so maybe I'll try being a villain.
